Title: A specialized flavone biosynthetic pathway has evolved in the medicinal plant, Scutellaria baicalensis

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern: expressed in roots; single CHI transcript identified, no novel root-specific isoform reported
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports pinocembrin formation but is not a specialized determinant of the root-specific 4′-deoxyflavone pathway
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Only one CHI transcript was identified in root RNA-seq, suggesting no novel CHI isoform for RSF biosynthesis. 2. In Nicotiana benthamiana, SbCLL-7 plus SbCHS-2 produced pinocembrin without requiring SbCHI, indicating endogenous CHI/spontaneous isomerization is sufficient. 3. Adding SbCHI did not substantially change pinocembrin production, so CHI is not a core specialized step in the evolved pathway.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq identification; transient pathway reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana
Common_Genes.Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter in transient expression constructs
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper concludes that chalcone isomerization may occur spontaneously or through the same CHI active in aerial tissues; SbCHI is not presented as a specialized root-evolved core determinant.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: cinnamic acid supplementation in transient tobacco assays; methyl jasmonate treatment discussed for pathway genes but not specifically for SbCHI
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA300475; SRP068883; SRS1263237; SRX1546377; SRR3123399
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Likely not a priority engineering target alone; endogenous CHI activity in hosts may suffice when reconstructing the pathway

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: 4′-deoxy root-specific flavones including chrysin, baicalein, baicalin, wogonin, and wogonoside
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream; committed flavone-forming step from pinocembrin to chrysin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts pinocembrin to chrysin by NADPH-dependent dehydrogenation/desaturation typical of cytochrome P450 FNSII enzymes
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor of baicalein, baicalin, wogonin, and wogonoside
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes synthesis of 4′-deoxyflavones; silencing strongly reduces baicalin, baicalein, wogonoside, and wogonin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbFNSII-2 is highly expressed in roots and induced 4.8-fold by methyl jasmonate, matching root-specific flavone accumulation. 2. RNAi silencing markedly reduced baicalin, baicalein, wogonoside, and wogonin and caused accumulation of pinocembrin-O-glucuronide and reduced derivatives, demonstrating blockage at the flavone-forming step. 3. Recombinant SbFNSII-2 specifically converted pinocembrin to chrysin but did not use naringenin or eriodictyol. 4. Overexpression in Arabidopsis produced chrysin only when pinocembrin was supplied, proving in planta function and dependence on upstream pinocembrin supply. 5. In Nicotiana benthamiana, coexpression with SbCLL-7 and SbCHS-2 reconstructed chrysin production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi in hairy roots; yeast microsome enzyme assays; in vivo yeast feeding; Arabidopsis overexpression with substrate feeding; transient reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana; metabolite profiling by HPLC/LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: native expression analyzed; CaMV 35S promoter in Arabidopsis and tobacco expression assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named CYP93B25; likely arose by recent duplication/neofunctionalization from SbFNSII-1; more substrate-specific but lower catalytic activity than SbFNSII-1.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: methyl jasmonate treatment; pinocembrin feeding in Arabidopsis; cinnamic acid supplementation in tobacco reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: strongly root-enriched; much higher in roots than stems, leaves, and flowers; induced by MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for 4′-deoxyflavone formation from pinocembrin
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower catalytic activity than SbFNSII-1 despite specialized substrate selectivity
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA300475; SRP068883; SRS1263237; SRX1546377; SRR3123399
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential engineering gene for chrysin/4′-deoxyflavone production; should be stacked with SbCLL-7 and SbCHS-2 because endogenous hosts may lack pinocembrin supply

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: 4′-deoxy root-specific flavones including baicalein, baicalin, wogonin, and wogonoside
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: entry step bypassing C4H to supply cinnamoyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: activates cinnamic acid to cinnamoyl-CoA in a CoA ligase reaction
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for pinocembrin and downstream 4′-deoxyflavones
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for efficient biosynthesis of root-specific 4′-deoxyflavones; silencing lowers baicalin, baicalein, wogonoside, and wogonin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbCLL-7 is preferentially expressed in roots, consistent with RSF accumulation. 2. Recombinant SbCLL-7 is specific for cinnamic acid and shows much higher catalytic efficiency for this substrate than other SbCLLs tested. 3. RNAi silencing reduced levels of major root flavones, demonstrating requirement in the pathway. 4. Expression of SbCLL-7 alone in Arabidopsis did not produce pinocembrin, indicating host CHS cannot use cinnamoyl-CoA efficiently in vivo. 5. In Nicotiana benthamiana, coexpression with SbCHS-2 enabled pinocembrin formation and with SbFNSII-2 enabled chrysin production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression analysis; purified recombinant enzyme assays and kinetics; RNAi in hairy roots; Arabidopsis overexpression; transient reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: native expression analyzed; CaMV 35S promoter in heterologous expression/transient assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contains a C-terminal SKL peroxisomal targeting motif; phylogenetically related to At4CLL7 rather than AtCNL, suggesting convergent evolution of cinnamate-CoA ligase activity.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: methyl jasmonate treatment showed no significant induction; cinnamic acid supplementation in Arabidopsis/tobacco assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: highest in roots; lower in aerial organs; not significantly induced by MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key branch-entry/control step enabling C4H bypass and cinnamoyl-CoA supply
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Expression alone is insufficient in heterologous hosts lacking compatible CHS activity
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA300475; SRP068883; SRS1263237; SRX1546377; SRR3123399
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target for introducing pinocembrin/4′-deoxyflavone flux; should be stacked with SbCHS-2 and SbFNSII-2

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: 4′-deoxy root-specific flavones including chrysin, baicalein, baicalin, wogonin, and wogonoside
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: midstream; precursor-condensing step for pinocembrin chalcone formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: condenses cinnamoyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form pinocembrin chalcone, which is converted to pinocembrin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor pool for chrysin and downstream 4′-deoxyflavones
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required specialized CHS isoform for pinocembrin and chrysin production in the root-specific 4′-deoxyflavone pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbCHS-2 is highly expressed in roots, unlike SbCHS-1 which is flower-enriched. 2. Its expression pattern correlates with root-specific flavone accumulation and is strongly induced by methyl jasmonate. 3. In Nicotiana benthamiana, SbCLL-7 plus SbCHS-2 produced pinocembrin, showing SbCHS-2 can use cinnamoyl-CoA in vivo. 4. Coexpression of SbCLL-7, SbCHS-2, and SbFNSII-2 reconstructed chrysin production, identifying SbCHS-2 as a core specialized pathway enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq and qRT-PCR expression profiling; transient pathway reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: native expression analyzed; CaMV 35S promoter in transient expression assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highly similar to previously reported SbCHS2a/SbCHS-C and SbCHS2b, likely allelic forms; inferred to be a recently diverged specialized isoform.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: methyl jasmonate treatment; cinnamic acid supplementation in reconstruction assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: high in roots, very low in flowers; induced about 16-fold by MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: type III polyketide synthase, chalcone synthase family
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key specialized step for routing cinnamoyl-CoA into pinocembrin branch
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Specialized function inferred from pathway reconstruction; standalone enzymology with purified protein was not reported in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA300475; SRP068883; SRS1263237; SRX1546377; SRR3123399
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important stacking partner with SbCLL-7 and SbFNSII-2 for engineering 4′-deoxyflavone production

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: standard flavones such as apigenin/luteolin-type products; not root-specific 4′-deoxyflavones
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts flavanones to flavones through NADPH-dependent dehydrogenation/desaturation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: competes with or parallels specialized branch but not decisive for RSFs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Broad-specificity FNSII with little to no contribution to major root-specific 4′-deoxyflavone accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbFNSII-1 is expressed at relatively low levels across organs and is not induced by MeJA. 2. RNAi silencing of SbFNSII-1 had no detectable effect on the four major root-specific flavones. 3. Recombinant SbFNSII-1 converts pinocembrin, naringenin, and eriodictyol, preferring naringenin, consistent with a general flavone synthase role rather than a specialized root-specific function.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi in hairy roots; qRT-PCR; yeast microsome enzyme assays and kinetics
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named CYP93B24; likely ancestral/broad-specificity enzyme from which SbFNSII-2 diverged.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: low and broadly distributed across roots, stems, leaves, and flowers; no significant MeJA response
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing had no RSF phenotype, indicating limited value for specialized pathway engineering
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA300475; SRP068883; SRS1263237; SRX1546377; SRR3123399
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lower priority than SbFNSII-2 for 4′-deoxyflavone engineering
